title: Search Query DSL
description: Defines the root JSON DSL document used to describe a search query before it is translated to Apache Solr or Elasticsearch.

id.description: Optional randomly generated identifier for this query instance.

name.description: Required human-readable query name used in logs and diagnostics.

materialTypes.description: Required list of source or material types to fetch data from.

items.description: A source or material type identifier.

QueryNode.description: A recursive query node. A node with an empty field is a boolean/group node; if the field is equal to the name of a document type in the mapping, then the sub-queries in 'data' query the fields in the specified document type; if 'field' is empty, then the node is a leaf field query.
QueryNode.$comment: The field value determines the shape of data and whether occur/mm are allowed.

field.description: Field to query. If empty or equal to the name of a document type, then the 'data' field is expected to be an array of arrays, representing a boolean relationship of sub-queries or a nested query.

minimumMatch.description: Required amount of 'should' clauses that should match. Only allowed when 'field' is empty or equal to a document type name.

isNot.description: If true, then perform negation on this clause, i.e. require this query node to NOT match.

data.description: When 'field' is not empty, contains an object representing the query payload to match on the specified field. When 'field' is empty or equal to the name of a document type, contains an array of arrays representing a boolean sub-query. Each direct array under 'data' represents a 'should' clause, and within each sub-array, there are sub-query nodes which are treated with a 'must' relationship, i.e. every query node within a single sub-array should match. In the case of a nested query (when 'field' is equal to the name of a document type), all clauses under each sub-array must match on the same child document.

then.$comment: Boolean query - contains an array of arrays representing a boolean sub-query. Each direct array under 'data' represents a 'should' clause, and within each sub-array, there are sub-query nodes which are treated with a 'must' relationship, i.e. every query node within a single sub-array should match.

data.description: Child 'should' clauses for this boolean query.

else.$comment: Leaf query - contains an object representing the query to match on the specified field.

QueryPayload.description: Payload for a leaf field query.

TextQuery.description: Text query payload for matching string or free-text fields.

phrases.description: Text values to match. Always treated as phrases - if the text is analyzed to multiple tokens, the resulting query is a phrase query.

RangeQuery.description: Range query payload for matching either numeric values or datetime/timestamp values within bounds.

ExactQuery.description: Exact query payload for matching numeric, datetime/timestamp, or boolean values.

NumericExactQuery.description: Exact query payload for matching one or more numeric values.

DatetimeExactQuery.description: Exact query payload for matching one or more datetime or timestamp values.

values.description: Datetime or timestamp values to match exactly.

BooleanExactQuery.description: Exact query payload for matching one or more boolean values.

NumericRangeQuery.description: Range query payload for matching numeric values within bounds.

DatetimeRangeQuery.description: Range query payload for matching datetime or timestamp values within bounds.
